name: Marcus Rivera
chief_complaint: My stomach really hurts, it started around my belly button but now it's all down on the right side
onset_description: Started about 12 hours ago as a vague ache around the umbilicus, gradually shifted to the right lower quadrant over the last 4-5 hours. Getting worse.

abdominal_pain.description: Sharp, constant pain that got way worse when I moved or coughed
abdominal_pain.onset: 12 hours ago, migrated to RLQ 4-5 hours ago
abdominal_pain.character: started dull and achy, now sharp and constant
aggravating_factors: riding in the car over bumps
alleviating_factors: lying still with knees drawn up

nausea.description: I feel like I'm gonna throw up but I haven't yet

anorexia.description: I haven't been able to eat anything all day, just the thought of food makes me feel sick

fever.description: I feel a little warm but not like burning up or anything
alleviating_factors: took some Tylenol but it didn't help the belly pain

labs.CBC: WBC 14.8 (elevated, left shift with 82% neutrophils), Hgb 15.2, Plt 260
labs.BMP: Na 140, K 3.9, Cr 0.9, Glucose 102, BUN 14 - within normal limits
labs.urinalysis: Clear, no WBC, no RBC, no nitrites, no leukocyte esterase

medications: None regular, took Acetaminophen 500mg x2 about 6 hours ago

social_history.alcohol: Drinks beer on weekends, maybe 4-6 per weekend
social_history.occupation: Warehouse worker, lots of heavy lifting
social_history.living_situation: Lives with two roommates in an apartment
social_history.exercise: Plays recreational soccer twice a week, goes to the gym 3 times a week
social_history.diet: Eats a lot of fast food, admits to not cooking much

personality: stoic and tough, tries to downplay pain but clearly uncomfortable, a little embarrassed about coming to the ER
communication_style: Short answers, doesn't elaborate unless prompted. Says things like 'I'm fine' while visibly guarding his abdomen. Uses slang and casual language. Needs direct questions to open up.
pain_description_style: Rates pain lower than it appears. Says 'it's not that bad' while wincing. When pressed, admits it's the worst stomach pain he's ever had. Describes it as 'like someone stabbing me.'

critical_findings: Classic migration of pain from periumbilical to RLQ
Rebound tenderness and guarding pattern (pain worse with movement/coughing)
